slides.title: A YANG Module for Entitlement Inventory
YANG Data Model for Passive Network Inventory

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: The note page can be found here - https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-ivy?both

chat.author: Qiufang Ma
chat.text: Thanks, Mahesh.

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: a site will containmultiples levels of security racks

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: I think Swami makes a good point. Racks are passive inventory and it seems it would make more sense of the idenitiies to be defined there.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: @Deaniele, I think the point you are making is more of whether a device is secure or not. That can be derived by locating the device in a rack and whether the rack is secure or not.

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: there will be similar in all countries and can be found on the government security standards for each country normally

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: And sorry for munging your name.

chat.author: Qin Wu
chat.text: @Brad, can you provide some informative references, I feel these are mostly applied to enterprise network scenario.

chat.author: Qin Wu
chat.text: set boundary seems hard, but  categorize the passive device will be much easy.  so what are main categories of passive devices?

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: One way to handle this would be to say that this is base passive inventory model and it would have a set boundary. Other models such as microwave models could augment the base model.

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: @qin wu will provide some references i'll pick a few countries

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: i'll provide on git hub

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: in regards to the scope. I suppose I was looking at this as enabling operators capabilities that operators utilise today to optimise operations. for items such as class of a rack determines the type of security classified staff that is required for conducting assurance on that rack as well as the fact that they may need to get keys from else where. The addition stops the need to swivel chair for operation tasks.

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: Australian government dates are 2030. Far left hand side of the dates.

chat.author: Mohammad Boroon
chat.text: I agree with keeping passive inventory domain agnostic and call it base passive inventory, which can be used for domain specific extensions

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: What would you do with macsec at layer 2

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: And secure boot

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: Depends on what aspect of MacSec or Secure Boot.

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: If it is not part of inventory layer 0-3

chat.author: Brad Peters
chat.text: From an operator aspect in au where the dates are 2030 and not 2035 every telco is currently working on this topic. For their network. Jfi
